Obtain a city permit for sewer lateral work, including any street-cut encroachment permit if the connection point is under the public right-of-way.
For trench method: excavate along the full lateral run from the house to the city main connection.
For trenchless method: excavate two access pits and pull a new pipe through the existing line via pipe-bursting or install a cured-in-place liner.
Install new pipe (typically ABS or PVC) for the full lateral run.
Connect the new lateral to the house drain and to the city main per code.
Install or replace the cleanout access point at the property line.
Perform a water/pressure test on the new line.
Backfill and compact all excavated areas.
Restore any hardscape/landscape disturbed by excavation to a reasonable match.
Clean the work area and haul away debris.
Final inspection, including city sign-off on the main connection.
selections: Trench vs. trenchless method (based on site access and cost)
exclusions: Full hardscape/landscape replacement beyond a reasonable patch match
notes: Trenchless pipe-bursting or lining avoids tearing up a driveway or mature landscaping but requires the existing line to have a workable path and adequate diameter — confirm via camera before quoting.
Work at the public right-of-way connection typically requires city inspection and sign-off separate from the standard plumbing permit.

jobTypes.name: French Drain & Site Drainage
jobTypes.summary: Installation of a designed subsurface drainage system to move standing water away from the foundation or low points in the yard.
steps: Identify the source of standing water and the intended discharge point (street, dry well, or storm drain connection).
Excavate a trench sloped consistently toward the discharge point.
Line the trench with landscape fabric to prevent silt intrusion.
Apply a bed of gravel in the trench bottom.
Install perforated drain pipe in the trench, sloped for positive drainage.
Wrap the pipe and surrounding gravel fully in the landscape fabric.
Backfill the trench with additional gravel to a few inches below grade.
Cap the trench with soil, sod, or decorative rock per the customer's preference.
Install a catch basin at low points or downspout tie-ins as needed.
Test the system with a hose to confirm proper flow to the discharge point.
Clean the work area and haul away excess soil/debris.
selections: Discharge point (street, dry well, or storm drain, subject to local code)
Surface cap finish (soil, sod, or rock)
exclusions: Storm drain connection permit/fees if tying into the public system
Regrading of the broader yard beyond the drain path
notes: Some SoCal jurisdictions restrict draining directly to the street or a neighboring property — confirm the legal discharge point before excavation begins.
A French drain addresses subsurface/standing water; if the problem is roof water, address gutters and downspouts first (see Rain Gutters).

jobTypes.name: Skylight Install
jobTypes.summary: Installation of a new skylight into the roof and ceiling to bring natural light into a room with no exterior wall access.
steps: Locate the skylight position between roof rafters and ceiling joists, avoiding structural members where possible.
Cut the roof opening and frame the rough opening per the skylight manufacturer's specification.
Install roof flashing and the skylight curb per manufacturer instructions.
Set and secure the skylight unit to the curb.
Cut the corresponding ceiling opening below.
Frame and install a light shaft connecting the roof opening to the ceiling opening (for standard skylights) or install the tubular light kit (for tubular skylights).
Insulate the light shaft/tube per code.
Install drywall around the ceiling opening; tape, texture, and paint to match the surrounding ceiling.
Test the skylight and flashing with a hose for watertightness.
Clean the work area and haul away debris.
Final inspection.
selections: Skylight type: standard fixed/vented or tubular
inclusions: Skylight unit, flashing, and shaft framing
exclusions: Full ceiling repaint beyond the immediate patch area
notes: Flashing installation is the highest-risk step for future leaks — this is not an area to cut corners or substitute non-manufacturer flashing kits.
Patch-and-paint of the ceiling opening is a local match only.

jobTypes.name: Garage Door Replacement
jobTypes.summary: Removal of an old, damaged, or uninsulated garage door and installation of a new like-for-like or upgraded insulated door and opener.
steps: Disconnect and remove the existing garage door, tracks, and opener hardware as needed; haul away.
Inspect and repair the door opening framing/header as needed.
Install new vertical tracks and mounting hardware sized to the new door.
Install the new garage door panels per the customer's selected style and insulation rating.
Install new springs, cables, and rollers rated for the door weight.
Install or reconnect the garage door opener, including safety sensors.
Test balance, travel limits, and auto-reverse safety function.
Weatherstrip the door perimeter and threshold.
Clean the work area and haul away debris.
exclusions: Opener unit if not upgraded (reused existing unless replacement is scoped)
notes: California code requires auto-reverse safety sensors on all garage door openers — verify functional sensors on every install, even opener reuse.
